question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 removes `fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa` from GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
(the primary path to R3). TI-LFA backup for the primary interface is
no longer pre-programmed. Steady-state traffic still uses the primary
unchanged, but when the primary fails, sub-50ms protection is lost —
traffic blackholes until full IS-IS reconvergence (seconds). Negative-
ish case: no syslog, no adjacency drop, no route change until something
*else* fails. Pure protection-state misconfig.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R2, fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa (and per-prefix FRR) was removed from GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0, the primary path to R3.
Without per-prefix TI-LFA, IS-IS no longer pre-computes and pre-installs a loop-free backup path for the primary interface, so the change is operationally inert while the primary stays in service - steady-state forwarding still follows the primary unchanged. The consequence is latent: if the primary later fails, there is no pre-programmed backup to switch to, so traffic blackholes until full IS-IS reconvergence (seconds) instead of getting sub-50ms protection. Pure protection-state misconfig with no active fault.
Fix: re-enable fast-reroute per-prefix and fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa on R2's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario by design.
Pure protection-state misconfig: adjacency stays Up, route table
unchanged, no event fires. Diagnostic ground truth lives in
expected_state_diffs (pre-failure protection check).
expected_state_diffs: R2 `show running-config router isis LAB interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: no `fast-reroute per-prefix` and no `fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa` lines (baseline: both present).
Diff vs any other router's primary interface (e.g. R3 `show running-config router isis LAB interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`): the other end keeps both lines.
R2 `show isis fast-reroute detail` for 192.0.2.4/32: backup column reports `No backup` (baseline: backup via R6 with a labelled path).
R2 `show isis fast-reroute summary`: backup coverage % drops vs baseline. Operationally this is the signal a TI-LFA dashboard would surface.
Steady-state forwarding unchanged: `show route 192.0.2.4` still resolves via the primary; the gap only matters when the primary fails.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  no fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa
  no fast-reroute per-prefix

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  fast-reroute per-prefix
  fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa
